page: Amazon Web Services c3.4xlarge is a compute-optimized instance featuring 16 vCPUs and 8 physical cores powered by an Intel Xeon E5-2680 v2 processor running at 2.8 GHz. Built on the x86_64 architecture and managed via the Xen hypervisor, this instance allocates dedicated CPU resources with hyper-threading enabled. It is equipped with 30.0 GB of system memory, yielding 1.88 GB per core, and includes 320 GB of local NVMe SSD storage. Network connectivity is established with a baseline bandwidth of 2 Gbps.

Performance benchmarks reveal a distinct contrast between single-core and multi-core capabilities. Single-core execution is weak, with Geekbench single-core and stress-ng single-core metrics falling into the bottom 10% to 25% categories. However, multi-core performance is average, as demonstrated by Passmark CPU and Geekbench multi-core scores. Memory bandwidth for both cached and uncached operations consistently registers in the middle 50% tier.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, this third-generation instance offers a balanced resource density for multi-threaded workloads that benefit from local NVMe SSD storage, though it lacks modern instruction sets like AVX-512. It is suitable for multi-threaded legacy applications, static web serving, and basic compilation tasks, but is less efficient for single-threaded operations or modern LLM prompt processing.
description: Amazon Web Services c3.4xlarge is a compute-optimized instance featuring 16 vCPUs and 8 physical cores powered by an Intel Xeon E5-2680 v2 processor at 2.8 GHz. It includes 30.0 GB of memory, 320 GB of local NVMe SSD storage, and a 2 Gbps baseline network speed. Running on the Xen hypervisor, this x86_64 instance exhibits a clear performance tradeoff: single-core benchmarks are weak, placing in the bottom 25%, while multi-core performance and memory bandwidth remain in the average tier. Lacking modern instruction sets like AVX-512, it is best suited for legacy multi-threaded applications, static web serving, and software compilation where local NVMe storage is beneficial.
og_description: Amazon Web Services c3.4xlarge is a compute-optimized instance with 16 vCPUs, 30 GB RAM, and 320 GB NVMe SSD storage. It offers average multi-core performance but exhibits weak single-core capabilities.

tagline: An x86_64 compute-optimized instance featuring dedicated Intel Xeon cores, bundled local NVMe SSD storage, and balanced multi-core performance.
